help.title: User guide
help.intro: Gluestick Desktop is a graphical package manager for Windows. Search, install, update, and manage software without the command line.

sections.heading: Getting started
items: Type a keyword in the search box to find packages across your buckets.
Open a package and click Install to download and install it automatically.
The first bucket add or package install may need an internet connection.

sections.heading: Manage packages
items: The Installed view lists everything you have installed and flags available updates.
Use Uninstall to remove a package, or remove only inactive versions to reclaim space.
Pro: roll back to a previously installed version, or lock a version to skip updates.

sections.heading: Buckets
items: Buckets are package sources kept in sync with Git.
Add, remove, or refresh buckets from the Packages menu; a background scheduler checks them periodically.

sections.heading: Tools
items: Use Environment… in the Tools menu for step-by-step Git, 7-Zip, shim, and GitHub checks.
Configure a GitHub mirror and download concurrency from the Tools menu.
Open root directory reveals the ~/.glue data folder in File Explorer.

pro.title: Gluestick Desktop Pro
pro.intro: Pro manages your environment across six capabilities: sync, recipes, snapshots, compliance audit, sharing, and environment health.

deviceSync.title: Device Sync
deviceSync.desc: Sign in to push/pull environment snapshots across devices and restore on a new machine (requires network). Day-to-day local installs and management don't depend on the cloud

workspace.title: Recipes
workspace.desc: Declare a role environment (e.g. frontend / data) and align this machine to the target list in one click

snapshot.title: Snapshots & rollback
snapshot.desc: Auto-snapshot before install/uninstall/upgrade; roll back if something fails or breaks

complianceAudit.title: Compliance audit
complianceAudit.desc: Inventory installed software and export Markdown/Word compliance reports for review, asset records, and change reconciliation

teamCollab.title: Share
teamCollab.desc: Export recipes for others or import shared recipe files (org features coming later)

envHealth.title: Environment health
envHealth.desc: Dashboard and suggestions from the latest env check, recent failures, and available updates

recipe.title: Recipes
recipe.desc: Declare a role environment (e.g. frontend / data) and align this machine to the target list in one click

picker.title: Choose theme
picker.upgradeUnlock: Upgrade to Pro to unlock all themes

editor.importError: Cannot parse theme file — check JSON format

installDialog.title: Install package
installDialog.depsIntro: The following dependencies will be installed first, then the main package:
installDialog.archHint: System installers (e.g. .NET runtime) install into Windows; they appear under Installed as windowsdesktop-runtime-6.0, not .NET Framework.
installDialog.installModeSilentHint: Completes install without opening an external setup window (recommended).
installDialog.installModeInteractiveHint: Opens the external setup program; applies only to packages with installer.script in the manifest.
installDialog.installModeRequired: Choose silent or interactive install before continuing.
installDialog.forceHint: Force install (--force) will re-download and overwrite existing files of the same version.
installDialog.suggestHint: Optional components (suggest) will be offered after install; they are not installed automatically.
installDialog.installDone: Installation complete. Manifest is kept open for review.
installDialog.downloadUrlOverrideHint: URL differs from the bucket manifest; it will be saved locally when you confirm install.
installDialog.downloadUrlSavedHint: Using a saved local download URL override.

uninstallDialog.title: Uninstall package
uninstallDialog.inactiveNote: Only this historical version is removed; the active version and other installed versions are unaffected.

installedExt.favoritesFilterActive: Showing favorites only — click to show all
installedExt.favoritesEmpty: No favorite packages yet. Star packages in the list to find them quickly.
installedExt.sizeTitle: Installed file size for this package

versions.title: Version management
versions.subtitle: Older versions often remain on disk after upgrades — switch or roll back here
versions.lockTitle: Lock the active version to prevent automatic upgrades
versions.switchNote: This sets the active version; other installed versions are not deleted.
versions.hintMulti: Lists locally installed versions for rollback. Uninstall non-active versions to free space. To install other versions from a bucket, the manifest must support autoupdate and the version must be known (CLI: glue install package@version).
versions.hintSingle: Only one version installed. After upgrading here, the previous version is usually kept for rollback. Browse shows the bucket’s current version, not full install history.

storageExt.subtitle: View package cache; Clean orphans removes unreferenced files.
storageExt.emptyHint: Packages appear after install. Use “Clean orphans” for leftover files.
storageExt.purgeDialogBody: Remove {{name}}{{version}} from the cache index and delete unreferenced cache files.
storageExt.purgeDialogNote: Files still used by installed software will not be deleted.
storageExt.gcDialogBody: Scan the cache directory and delete entries not referenced by the cache index or installed software.
storageExt.gcDialogNote: Same as CLI glue cache gc. Files in use are not deleted. The task runs in the background after confirmation.

activityExt.subtitle: Recent installs, uninstalls, updates, and environment checks
activityExt.empty: No history in this time range
activityExt.clearIntro: Choose what to clear. This cannot be undone.

popular-essentials.title: Setup essentials
popular-essentials.summary: Cross-ecosystem must-haves for a new Windows setup
popular-essentials.description: Curated from Chocolatey, WinGet, and Scoop popularity — version control, terminals, runtimes, and everyday utilities.

popular-browsers.title: Browsers
popular-browsers.description: The most installed mainstream and privacy-focused browsers across package managers.

popular-communication.title: Communication & notes
popular-communication.description: Chat, meetings, notes, and password management — top picks from WinGet and Chocolatey.

popular-developer-cli.title: Developer CLI toolkit
popular-developer-cli.summary: Modern terminal tools and cloud CLIs
popular-developer-cli.description: Modern terminal tools, fuzzy search, and cloud/container CLIs — frequent Scoop community picks.

popular-media.title: Media & creative
popular-media.description: Playback, recording, image/video editing, and screenshot tools in one bundle.

popular-security.title: Security & remote access
popular-security.summary: Passwords, malware defense, and network tools
popular-security.description: Password management, malware defense, packet analysis, and remote access tools.

vibe-coding.title: Vibe Coding
vibe-coding.description: For AI-native workflows — switch models and coding assistants quickly in the terminal.

game-dev.title: Game Dev
game-dev.description: Game engine and 3D modeling tools for 2D/3D prototypes and asset creation.

cad-3d.title: CAD / 3D
cad-3d.description: Open-source CAD suite for mechanical design, 3D printing, and engineering drawings.

database-dev.title: Databases
database-dev.description: Common relational, in-memory, and document databases for local development.
